name: Tvashtr
description: Tvashtr, also known as Vishwakarma, is a Hindu deity revered as the divine craftsman, architect, and artisan of the gods. He is the originator of all crafts and skills, responsible for creating the weapons, tools, and vehicles used by the deities. Tvashtr is often associated with the creation of the universe and the shaping of forms from primordial matter. He is considered the father of Saranyu, the wife of Surya, and the grandfather of the Ashvins and Yama. He possesses immense creative power and technical expertise, capable of crafting extraordinary objects and structures. Tvashtr is also known for his role in granting boons and curses, and his actions often have significant consequences in the mythological narratives. While sometimes benevolent, he can also be portrayed as envious or resentful, particularly in relation to Indra. His skill and artistry are unmatched, making him a vital figure in the Hindu pantheon, representing the divine ability to create, transform, and shape the world. He is associated with metalworking, carpentry, and all forms of craftsmanship, embodying the creative force that brings order and beauty to the cosmos. He is also linked to fertility and procreation, suggesting a broader role in the creation of life itself.
subtitle: Divine Craftsman and Architect of Gods

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Vajra
keyMyths.description: Tvashtr crafted the Vajra, Indra's powerful thunderbolt weapon, from the bones of the sage Dadhichi after he sacrificed himself. This weapon was instrumental in Indra's victory over the demon Vritra.

keyMyths.title: Tvashtr's Envy of Indra
keyMyths.description: Tvashtr created Soma, a potent drink, for Indra, but felt resentful when Indra consumed too much. He then created Vritra, a demon, to challenge Indra's power, leading to a great cosmic battle.

keyMyths.title: The Marriage of Saranyu
keyMyths.description: Tvashtr's daughter, Saranyu, married Surya, the Sun god. However, she found his heat unbearable and created a shadow replica of herself, Chhaya, to take her place while she escaped.
